channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: Four Member States challenge Poland’s nicotine pouch restrictions
item.description: When four Member States challenge Poland’s nicotine pouch restrictions, the issue is no longer just national policy. It becomes a test of proportionality, evidence and whether the EU can regulate new nicotine products without pushing them into a prohibitionist corner. Nicotine pouches should be controlled responsibly, but restrictions need to reflect relative risk and avoid closing legal pathways away from smoking. A single-market dispute can also be a public health warning. (Published on July 22, 2026bypouchforum.eu)

item.title: Educating Brazil
item.description: Brazil does not need another lesson in fear. It needs a serious conversation about evidence, risk and what happens when safer nicotine products are pushed out of legal reach. Education should help people understand why smoking is uniquely dangerous and how non-combustible alternatives differ from cigarettes. Without that clarity, policy can end up defending the very product it claims to defeat. (Published on July 22, 2026byplanetofthevapes.co.uk)

item.title: The Warning Light
item.description: Persistent smoking should be understood not only as an individual behaviour, but also as an indicator of policy limitations. When existing interventions fail to reach certain groups of smokers, harm reduction becomes an important complement to prevention and cessation. Safer nicotine products may reduce exposure to the toxicants associated with combustion, particularly among adults who do not quit through conventional routes. The warning signal is therefore not the existence of alternative nicotine use, but the failure to integrate risk-proportionate strategies into tobacco control. (Published on July 22, 2026bydisobedientmargins.com)
